rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH PHD VS. BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2024, line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ITS BOARD OF APPEALS, PLANNING DEPARTMENT's DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Govt. Code 65009(c)(1). = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WAN FEN TAN VS. RICARDO IAN SUMMERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Court To Appoint Contractor Pursuant To The Settlement Agreement Between Plaintiff And Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2024, line 3. PLAINTIFF WAN TAN For Court To Appoint Contractor Pursuant To The Settlement Agreement Between Plaintiff And Defendant. GRANTED. The contractor to "cure the violations" per the Settlement Agreement shall be Cheng Chang Construction Company. Within 10 business days of the DBI inspection report/notification regarding the cure work, the parties to file and notice for hearing a compliance motion, informing the Court of the status of abatement of the violations. If DBI does not find that the violation was cured, Plaintiff must submit a supplemental bid covering any outstanding issues as identified by the DBI. Defendant's supplemental declaration filed on August 12, 2024 is stricken. The filing was untimely and unresponsive to the sole purpose of the filing to reflect their chosen contractor/bid. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YONG SEN LU ET AL VS. KEVIN WAI LEONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2024, line 5. DEFENDANT KEVIN LEONG , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE SUI NGAN LEE LEONG TRUST, FENG JIN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that any claim in the complaint arise out of protected activity of service of the Notice. Plaintiff's claims arise, inter alia, out of Defendant's decision to demand the rent in violation of CC 1942.4 and demand rent for the months for which Defendant had previously acknowledged the receipt of payment. While these decisions and breaches of the law are evidenced by the notices, the service of the notices does not form the basis of the claim. Since the first prong has not been met, the Court will not address the second prong. The Court notes that the complaint is inartfully pleaded and is confusing. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
